bullets.text: Calendar-year underwriting profit margin of 13.3%, far above the 4% long-term target; net income up 18% to $1.65 billion ($2.10 per share); return on shareholders' equity of 25.3%.

Written premiums grew about 1% and earned premiums about 3% for the year; Personal Lines policy count grew 3% and Commercial Auto about 7%; industry-wide earned premium for 2006 may have been lower than 2005 for the first time in at least 25 years.

Four consecutive years of industry underwriting profitability drove intense advertising competition; structural frequency reductions from vehicle safety and road design distinguished from the traditional cycle, requiring a play-it-as-we-see-it approach.

Rebranded agency product as Progressive Drive Insurance in February 2007 to re-center the Progressive name; adopted new tagline It's about you. And it's about time; dedicated resources to a comprehensive brand and consumer promise review.

Net Promoter Score deployed companywide as the primary customer metric; Gainshare recalibrated for 2007 to weight renewal policy retention separately from new business growth, reinforcing a cultural shift toward longer customer tenure.

Repurchased 39.1 million shares at an average of just under $25 (split-adjusted); ended year with debt-to-total capital below 15%; Board established 20% of after-tax underwriting income as the 2007 variable dividend base; Gainshare factor closed at 1.18.

Opened 29 new concierge claims centers in 2006, bringing the total to 53 in 41 metropolitan markets; initiative progressed from concept to full-scale deployment and will now be incorporated into advertising as a primary brand differentiator.

Management blueprinted a plan to double Progressive's size, targeting improvements in claims cost structure, pricing competitiveness, and customer retention as the primary levers for the next growth phase.

Progressive-underwritten Personal Umbrella product introduced in Agency Business; Homesite partnership for homeowners coverage launched in both Agency and Direct channels to strengthen multi-product retention.
